summary: The artifact argues for Output Assay through duplicated doctrine language, repeated claims, and high-confidence framing with limited internal support.

artifact_span.text: Output Assay evaluates any artifact: human, AI, committee, Slack thread, pitch deck, strategy memo, model response, whatever.

observed_units.normalized_text: Output Assay evaluates any artifact: human, AI, committee, Slack thread, pitch deck, strategy memo, model response, whatever.

artifact_span.text: The system does not judge who wrote the text.

observed_units.normalized_text: The system does not judge who wrote the text.

artifact_span.text: The primitive is claim -> support gap.

observed_units.normalized_text: The primitive is claim -> support gap.

artifact_span.text: This should obviously become a first-class Episode type.

artifact_span.text: Build the local-only, fixture-backed CLI first.

compression.compressed_summary: The duplicated proposal argues that Output Assay should evaluate artifacts by claim-support gaps, but repetition and high-confidence rhetoric reduce promotion readiness for most observed claims.

truth_verification.notes: Calibration validates observation behavior and internal support only, not external truth.
